jokes.text: We interrupt the triumph over COVID-19 for a special report. A new virus... - Oh, for Pete's sake.
jokes.punchline: Immediate interruption with news of another virus and exasperated reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama timing - sets up the familiar COVID experience then immediately subverts it. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: EXPLOVID-23
jokes.setup: Need to name a new virus
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'explosive' and 'COVID'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that captures the explosive anger symptom while parodying COVID variant naming conventions

jokes.text: As well as a mild cough, which I don't have. I'm fine.
jokes.punchline: Reporter immediately demonstrating the symptom while denying it
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony but well-executed. Familiar COVID-era behavior translated to future setting.

jokes.text: Bye, Mom! Bye, Dad! Love ya! - Feh. Always with the love. - We already hugged you. Now, we gotta talk about it?
jokes.punchline: Parents' exasperated reaction to emotional display
jokes.explanation: Relatable family dynamic humor with good character voices. The escalation from 'always with the love' to 'gotta talk about it' is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Let's move, you sofa snails! We've got a delivery!
jokes.punchline: 'Sofa snails' as creative insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth creative insult. Good alliteration and visual imagery. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Bill Nye, the Science Head!
jokes.punchline: Bill Nye the Science Guy becomes 'Science Head' in the future
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective future twist on familiar pop culture reference. Economy is strong.

jokes.text: Thank you for this tremendously dubious honor.
jokes.punchline: 'Tremendously dubious' - backhanded gratitude
jokes.explanation: Dry wit appropriate for scientist character. The oxymoron of 'tremendously dubious' works well.

jokes.text: After what seemed like a thousand years of COVID... - But was actually more!
jokes.punchline: Correction that it was actually longer than the hyperbole
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of hyperbole. The correction makes the impossible literally true, perfect for sci-fi setting.

jokes.text: I'm using the principles of aerodynamics to get the hell outta here!
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation for running away
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry - trying to sound smart while doing something cowardly. The contrast between 'principles of aerodynamics' and 'get the hell outta here' is excellent.

jokes.text: An anger pandemic? Not to point fingers, but Leela's spreading it. I am not, you jerks! I'm just running a couple degrees angrier than normal.
jokes.setup: Fry accusing Leela of spreading anger
jokes.punchline: Leela's immediate angry response proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff with Leela immediately demonstrating the behavior she's denying. Her euphemistic 'couple degrees angrier' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Good news, infecter-inos! I've developed a test to see who's got the virus and who's just naturally mean.
jokes.punchline: 'Infecter-inos' portmanteau and distinguishing virus from natural meanness
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth wordplay with 'infecter-inos' and the practical distinction between infected and naturally mean is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That looks kinda fun. I can't wait to do it hundreds of times!
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic masochistic response
jokes.explanation: Very Fry-like response - missing the obvious negative and being weirdly enthusiastic about pain.

jokes.text: This can't be right, Professor! Your test stinks, and so does your ugly face! - Oh no! She's got it!
jokes.setup: Leela reacting angrily to test results
jokes.punchline: Professor's obvious conclusion that she has the anger virus
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-payoff structure. Leela's over-the-top insult immediately proves the test correct.

jokes.text: Stupid, stinkin', lousy, no good, son of a...
jokes.punchline: String of increasingly angry epithets getting cut off
jokes.explanation: Standard angry character escalation. Gets the job done but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: I am therefore issuing an executive aroo that everyone wear a mask over their ears to prevent the transmission of upsetting remarks.
jokes.setup: Nixon addressing the anger virus spread
jokes.punchline: 'Executive aroo' and masks over ears instead of mouth/nose
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke hitting multiple levels - Nixon's signature 'aroo', absurdist logic of ear masks for angry words, and perfect pandemic satire. Highly quotable and rewatchable.

jokes.text: Thanks, fellas. Since I don't hear any questions, this prrress conference is adjourned!
jokes.setup: Nixon with ear masks preventing him from hearing
jokes.punchline: Ending press conference because he can't hear questions due to his own policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the ear mask joke. Nixon's obliviousness to the consequences of his own policy is spot-on political satire.

jokes.text: Dwight, stop Gloom-bombing your dad's meeting! And put on some britches!
jokes.punchline: 'Gloom-bombing' as futuristic Zoom-bombing plus naked child
jokes.explanation: Great pandemic-era reference updated for the future. 'Gloom-bombing' is clever wordplay and the naked child adds to the chaos.

jokes.text: Professor, you're a teddy bear! I assure you I am not. Aw...
jokes.setup: Amy commenting on Professor's appearance with filters
jokes.punchline: Professor's indignant denial followed by Amy's continued 'aww'
jokes.explanation: Visual gag relying on video filters making the cranky Professor look cute. His indignant response is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Everything we do is a violation of health and safety standards!
jokes.setup: Hermes objecting to in-person work on safety grounds
jokes.punchline: Professor pointing out their entire operation is unsafe
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the show's premise - Planet Express is inherently dangerous. Good self-awareness and very quotable.

jokes.text: You know, I heard there's not really even a virus at all. It's just a big hoax, like the moon landing. - You were on the moon last week! You grew up on Mars! That's what they want you to think!
jokes.punchline: Fry pointing out obvious contradictions, Scruffy doubling down
jokes.explanation: Excellent pandemic-era conspiracy theory satire adapted to sci-fi setting. The absurdity of denying space travel while living in space is perfect.

jokes.text: EXPLOVID victims move at way too normal a speed to be zombies.
jokes.punchline: Professor's practical objection based on speed
jokes.explanation: Great scientific pedantry from Farnsworth. The idea that zombie speed is the determining factor is perfectly absurd yet logical.

jokes.text: There are all speeds of zombies out there! Fast ones. Slow ones. Brisk shufflers.
jokes.setup: Professor dismissing zombies based on speed
jokes.punchline: Hermes' classification system including 'brisk shufflers'
jokes.explanation: 'Brisk shufflers' is a delightful addition to zombie taxonomy. The bureaucratic precision is very Hermes.

jokes.text: But voodoo didn't stop believing in them.
jokes.setup: Professor saying people stopped believing in voodoo
jokes.punchline: Hermes reversing the phrase about voodoo believing in people
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that flips the expected phrase. Very economical and captures Hermes' mystical side perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh shoot. I forgot to pick up a goat! Dwight, go across the hall and borrow goat from Mrs. Wilson.
jokes.punchline: Casually asking to borrow a goat from neighbor
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist humor - treating borrowing a goat like borrowing sugar. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You must listen to me 'cause I grew up in a voodoo family, and zombie-ism killed my father! Then brought him back to life!
jokes.setup: LaBarbara's serious warning about zombies killing her father
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction that he was brought back to life
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - builds dramatic tension then deflates it perfectly. The logical contradiction of zombie problems is well-executed.

jokes.text: What? It's my emotional support gun.
jokes.punchline: Gun justified as emotional support item
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of emotional support animal policies taken to absurd extreme. Very economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Hand sanitizer? - No thanks. - Eh, more for me.
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant treating sanitizer refusal as personal gain
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. The idea of hoarding hand sanitizer for personal use is mildly absurd.

jokes.text: my city's most prominent egghead has developed a vaccine that will end my terrible poll numbers once and for all!
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's about poll numbers, not public health
jokes.explanation: Good political satire - politician's honest admission of self-interest. The casual corruption is well-delivered.

jokes.text: Thanks to cutting-edge RNA technology, my vaccine is as potent as it is untested.
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's completely untested
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth - presenting obvious flaws as selling points. The casual admission of no testing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Mine makes your arm shrivel and fall off! - Big deal! Mine comes with a futuristic way of knowing exactly who's been vaccinated and who hasn't, a small scrap of easily lost paper.
jokes.setup: Scientists competing with increasingly bad side effects
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's 'futuristic' solution is just paper cards
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the competition ending with perfect anti-climax. The 'futuristic' paper cards perfectly satirize vaccine cards.

jokes.text: I made your favorite, Leela. Chicken-shaped steak! "Wow, this is great, Fry!" Is what I'd say if this pan sauce weren't so bland!
jokes.setup: Fry proudly presenting meal to infected Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela's backhanded compliment structure ending in insult
jokes.explanation: Good structure with the fake-out compliment. Shows Leela's infection through her inability to be nice even when she tries.

jokes.text: That's a feature, not a bug.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about vaccine magnetism side effect
jokes.punchline: Tech industry phrase defending obvious flaw
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective use of familiar tech phrase. Gets the job done without being exceptional.

jokes.text: That's a feature, not a bug.
jokes.punchline: Tech industry terminology applied to side effects
jokes.explanation: Standard tech joke application but works in context

jokes.text: The sterility is essential! Studies show that kids are what make people angriest! Everyone hates kids!
jokes.punchline: Professor enthusiastically defending sterility as beneficial
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly suited to Farnsworth's character. The logical leap from anger to anti-child vaccine is absurd but well-delivered.

jokes.text: Dr. Banjo of The Dr. Banjo Experience Podcast Experience Hour.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous podcast name with redundant 'Experience'
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of pretentious podcast naming conventions. The redundancy is the main joke.

jokes.text: how do you respond to rumors I'm starting that there are 5G chips in your vaccine?
jokes.punchline: Admitting to creating the rumor he's reporting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant media satire about manufactured controversy. Great self-awareness about disinformation.

jokes.text: I would simply reassure you that the 5G chips are safe and purely for surveillance purposes.
jokes.punchline: Confirming conspiracy while calling it reassuring
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - confirms the conspiracy theory while presenting it as reassuring. Classic Professor obliviousness.

jokes.text: Tushy my ass!
jokes.setup: Passenger telling captain to sit his 'tushy' down
jokes.punchline: Captain's confused angry response mixing body parts
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay confusion. The anger makes the malapropism funnier but it's not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Hello, madam. Is this the Voodoo HQ? - Shh. You do voodoo? - You misconstrue. Who do voodoo at the HQ? - Entendu.
jokes.punchline: Extended wordplay on 'voodoo' and 'who do'
jokes.explanation: Clever extended wordplay that builds nicely. The French 'entendu' (understood) is a nice touch. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The Key of Bones, the Hanging Man, the Man Being Crushed by a Big Beignet, and the Man Being Beaten by the Saints Mascot.
jokes.punchline: Mixing mystical cards with New Orleans-specific absurd ones
jokes.explanation: Excellent blend of traditional tarot mysticism with New Orleans-specific humor. The beignet and Saints mascot cards are brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: These vaccine fascists are taking away our freedom! I might as well be in a cage at the zoo, like my aunt!
jokes.punchline: Comparing situation to aunt who's apparently in a zoo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that starts with familiar anti-vaccine rhetoric then veers into personal absurdity. The casualness of having a zoo aunt is funny.

jokes.text: What else should I post, Dad? - Who cares? The crazier the better. Tell 'em worm medicine helps!
jokes.setup: Family discussion about social media posting
jokes.punchline: Alien father coaching on spreading misinformation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal of disinformation source. Great satire about foreign interference and casual malice.

jokes.text: Sweet andouille of St. Louis! A robot made of bones!
jokes.punchline: Food-based exclamation mixing Louisiana cuisine with wrong city
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes exclamation with food reference. The geographical confusion (andouille with St. Louis) adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Je m'appelle Bonebot. Been bones-keepin' this boneyard for three bone-dred years.
jokes.punchline: Excessive bone-based puns in speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate puns but fairly predictable. 'Bone-dred' is the cleverest part. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: Probably 'cause I got no eyes. Come on! I'll give you a ride.
jokes.setup: Bonebot admitting he never looked around
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he has no eyes
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of obvious physical limitation. The casual transition to offering a ride is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: This place gimme the heebie-jeebies, and not the boogie-woogie kind!
jokes.punchline: Distinguishing between types of heebie-jeebies
jokes.explanation: Clever distinction between scary and musical heebie-jeebies

jokes.text: Barbados Slim?! - People Magazine's Sexiest Man Alive. Come in, Hermes! Make yourself feel inadequate.
jokes.setup: Hermes surprised to see his rival
jokes.punchline: Barbados immediately bragging and insulting Hermes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barbados Slim introduction - immediately establishing his arrogance and Hermes' insecurity. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You stay away from her! She's married to me now! You two are mostly ancient history.
jokes.punchline: 'Mostly ancient history' - undermining his own confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hermes' insecurity. 'Mostly' ancient history reveals his lingering doubts.

jokes.text: She goes by the name of... LaBarbara. And she was good at voodoo, too.
jokes.setup: Talking about voodoo expert he studied with
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's Hermes' wife with suggestive pause
jokes.explanation: Good relationship tension setup with typical Slim bragging

jokes.text: Sweet potatoes of Barbados! You don't see a lot of angry, shouting zombies, but it would explain a lot.
jokes.punchline: Food-based exclamation plus zombie analysis
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with location-specific food exclamation. Thoughtful zombie analysis.

jokes.text: What dark, well-lit sorcery is this?
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic description of 'dark, well-lit' sorcery
jokes.explanation: Excellent oxymoron that's both mystical and practical. Very economical and quotable. Shows Hermes' bureaucratic mind.

jokes.text: It's a highly contagious Omicron variant. - You're a contagious variant! - Go back where you came from, you Omi-clods.
jokes.punchline: Degrading to playground insults including 'Omi-clods'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from science to anger to name-calling. 'Omi-clods' is a clever portmanteau. Shows virus affecting aliens too.

jokes.text: thus inoculating against future infection. - Precisely. You got some serious mental pecs, husband.
jokes.punchline: LaBarbara's 'mental pecs' compliment
jokes.explanation: 'Mental pecs' is a brilliant metaphor combining intellectual and physical strength. Very quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: My second favorite kind. - Hey, cut that out! Why didn't I get a wink for my mental pecs?
jokes.punchline: Barbados jealously demanding recognition for his mental pecs
jokes.explanation: Good callback to mental pecs joke. Shows Barbados' narcissism and need for attention. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: Ouchie! I was, uh, promised a lollipop. Uh, here. Mm, tastes a bit like wig glue.
jokes.punchline: Childish complaint followed by bizarre lollipop flavor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Fry's childishness. Wig glue flavor is appropriately weird for voodoo setting.

jokes.text: After all, any sufficiently advanced magic is indistinguishable from science.
jokes.punchline: Hermes flipping Arthur C. Clarke's famous quote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of Clarke's law. Perfectly captures the episode's theme and Hermes' character arc. Highly quotable and intellectually satisfying.
